column6: how to grow papaya using water soluble fertilizer give me fertigation schedulePapaya responds very well to drip fertigation with water-soluble NPK, and a weekly schedule split by growth stage works best.[1][2] Below is a practical schedule you can adapt for South Indian conditions and ~200:200:400 g NPK/plant/year target.[3][4]## Basic assumptions- Plant spacing: 1.8 m × 1.8 m (about 3000 plants/ha).[4]- Irrigation: 4–8 L water/plant/day by drip depending on season and soil (lower in cool/rainy, higher in hot).[5]- Fertigation frequency: 2–3 times/week; quantities below are per **week** per plant.> Tip: Use common water-soluble grades like 19-19-19, 0-52-34 (MKP), 13-0-45 or 0-0-50 (MOP), calcium nitrate, magnesium sulfate, and a micronutrient mix.[6][1]***## Annual nutrient target via fertigationResearch in India shows good yield with 75–100% of around **250:250:500 g NPK/plant/year** through water-soluble fertigation.[1][7]To keep it simple, use:- **N:** 200 g/plant/year  - **P₂O₅:** 200 g/plant/year  - **K₂O:** 400 g/plant/year[3][4]This will be split over 10–12 months through drip.***## Stage-wise fertigation schedule (per plant)### 1) Establishment (0–2 months after planting)Goal: Rooting + initial vegetative growth.- Weekly target per plant:    - N: 2–3 g    - P₂O₅: 3–4 g    - K₂O: 2 g  Example using 19-19-19 + MKP:- 19-19-19: 10–12 g/plant/week (gives ~2 g N, 2 g P₂O₅, 2 g K₂O).[6]- 0-52-34 (MKP): 5–6 g/plant/week (adds ~3 g P₂O₅, 2 g K₂O).[8]Split this over 2–3 fertigations/week (e.g., Mon–Wed–Fri).***### 2) Vegetative growth (2–5 months)Goal: Strong canopy; slightly K-biased.- Weekly target per plant:    - N: 4–5 g    - P₂O₅: 3–4 g    - K₂O: 6–7 g  Example mix per plant per week:- 19-19-19: 15–18 g (≈3 g N, 3 g P₂O₅, 3 g K₂O).[6]- 13-0-45 or 0-0-50: 8–10 g (≈4–5 g K₂O).[2]Also:- Magnesium sulfate: 3–4 g/week (especially on sandy soils).  - Micronutrient WSF mix: 1–2 g/week, or foliar 0.5–1 g/L every 20–30 days.[3][9]***### 3) Flowering initiation (5–7 months)Goal: Strong flowering; more P and K, slightly reduce N.- Weekly target per plant:    - N: 4 g    - P₂O₅: 5–6 g    - K₂O: 8–9 g  Example mix per plant per week:- 19-19-19: 12–15 g (≈2.5–3 g each NPK).[6]- 0-52-34 (MKP): 8–10 g (≈4–5 g P₂O₅, 3–4 g K₂O).[8]- 13-0-45 / 0-0-50: 4–5 g (extra K).[2]Plus:- Boron: 0.2–0.3 g B/plant/week or foliar borax 0.2–0.3% every 30–40 days.[9]- Zinc: 0.5–1 g chelated Zn/plant/week or foliar ZnSO₄ 0.5%.[3][9]***### 4) Heavy fruiting (7–14 months)Goal: Fruit size, sweetness, colour; high K, moderate N.- Weekly target per plant:    - N: 3–4 g    - P₂O₅: 3–4 g    - K₂O: 10–12 g  Example mix per plant per week:- 19-19-19: 8–10 g (≈1.5–2 g each NPK).[6]- 0-52-34: 5–6 g (≈3 g P₂O₅, 2–3 g K₂O).[8]- 13-0-45 / 0-0-50: 10–12 g (≈9–10 g K₂O).[2]Also:- Calcium nitrate: 5–6 g/plant/week to reduce blossom-end rot, improve firmness.[8][10]- Magnesium sulfate: 4–5 g/plant/week.  Stop or reduce N if plants are too vegetative (very large soft leaves, less flowering).***## How to implement via tank / venturi### 1) Decide area and tank concentrationExample: 1000 plants on one irrigation block.- Suppose for fruiting stage you need per plant per week:    - 19-19-19: 10 g    - 0-52-34: 6 g    - 0-0-50: 10 g  For 1000 plants per **week**:- 19-19-19: 10 kg  - 0-52-34: 6 kg  - 0-0-50: 10 kg  If you fertigate 3 days/week, divide by 3:- Per fertigation (per 1000 plants):    - 19-19-19: ~3.3 kg    - 0-52-34: ~2 kg    - 0-0-50: ~3.3 kg  Dissolve in 200–300 L water and inject over 45–60 minutes into the drip set.[11][1]***## Irrigation + fertigation tips- Keep EC of fertigation solution below about 2–2.5 dS/m to avoid salt burn (measure with EC meter).[11][1]- Always irrigate 15–20 minutes with plain water, then fertigation, then finish with 10–15 minutes plain water to flush lines.  - Avoid fertigation on very hot afternoons; prefer early morning or evening.  - Maintain 25 kg FYM + 1 kg neem cake/plant at planting and annually to improve soil structure and reduce salt stress.[3][4]***## If you share details, schedule can be tightenedIf you tell:- Variety (Red Lady, Taiwan, Co-7, etc.)  - Age of current crop (in months)  - Spacing and how many plants per drip zone  - Whether you already applied any basal NPK/FYM  a precise gram-per-plant schedule in table form (month-wise) can be given for your exact field.Citations:[1] Standardisation of fertigation in papaya for higher productivity and profitability https://jhs.iihr.res.in/index.php/jhs/article/view/2133[2] FERTIGATION IMPROVES FRUIT YIELD AND QUALITY ... https://ishs.org/ishs-article/851_56/[3] Papaya Cultivation Practices For A Bountiful Harvest - BigHaat https://www.bighaat.com/kisan-vedika/blogs/papaya-cultivation-practices-for-a-bountiful-harvest[4] Manuring & Fertilization - Papaya https://nhb.gov.in/pdf/fruits/papaya/pap006.pdf[5] Papaya - Irrigation https://nhb.gov.in/pdf/fruits/papaya/pap005.pdf[6] [PDF] Effect of Drip Fertigation on Growth and Yield of Papaya (Carica ... https://www.cabidigitallibrary.org/doi/pdf/10.5555/20193504091[7] Fertigation Impact on Papaya Yield | PDF | Fertilizer | Mulch - Scribd https://www.scribd.com/document/372672843/Effect-of-fertigation-and-mulching-on-yield-and-yield-attributes-studies-in-papaya-under-South-Gujarat-conditions[8] How to increase Papaya Production & Profitability in Farm? https://utkarshagro.com/blogs/blogs/how-to-increase-papaya-production-profitability-in-farm[9] Performance of papaya variety 'Surya' under fertigation and foliar ... https://horizonepublishing.com/journals/index.php/PST/article/view/1194[10] Best Fertilizer for Papaya: Growing Guide for Success https://growplant.org/blog/best-fertilizer-for-papaya/[11] FERTIGATION IMPROVES FRUIT YIELD AND QUALITY OF PAPAYA https://www.ishs.org/ishs-article/851_56[12] IJTA - Serials Publications https://serialsjournals.com/abstract/80153_18.pdf[13] papaya drip fertilizer schedule - पपई पीक ड्रीप खत व्यवस्थापन https://www.nutris.in/papaya-drip-fertilizer-schedule[14] Fertiliser for Papaya Tree: Best NPK Feeding Guide for Australian ... https://completegrow.com.au/garden-plant-care-home/fertiliser-for-papaya-tree/[15] [PDF] Papaya Schedule 2023.cdr - YK Laboratories https://yklaboratories.com/assets/img/Schedule/PapayaSchedule2023%20(1).pdf
